explanation: Usnea barbata (old man's beard) lichen extract provides usnic acid and lichen polyphenols with potent antimicrobial, antifungal, and antioxidant properties. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations and used in protective and purifying formulas for its usnic acid antimicrobial and antioxidant content.

Vaccinium angustifolium (lowbush blueberry) fruit extract provides anthocyanins, pterostilbene, and polyphenol antioxidants with potent free-radical-scavenging and anti-inflammatory properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its dense anthocyanin antioxidant content.

Vaccinium macrocarpon (cranberry) fruit extract provides A-type proanthocyanidins, anthocyanins, vitamin C, and resveratrol with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and antioxidant formulas for its unique proanthocyanidin and polyphenol content.

Vaccinium macrocarpon fruit extract is cranberry extract providing A-type proanthocyanidins, anthocyanins, and polyphenol antioxidants — the same ingredient as Vaccinium Macrocarpon (Cranberry) Fruit Extract, listed without the common name parenthetical. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and brightening formulas for its antioxidant content.

The asterisk indicates organic certification; Vaccinium macrocarpon fruit juice is organic cranberry juice providing anthocyanins, vitamin C, and proanthocyanidin antioxidants in a water-soluble form for use in water-phase cosmetic formulas. It is well-tolerated and used in antioxidant and brightening formulas for its organic fruit-derived antioxidant content.

Vaccinium myrtillus (bilberry) fruit extract is one of the richest sources of anthocyanins in the plant kingdom, providing delphinidin, cyanidin, and malvidin glycosides with exceptional free-radical-scavenging, capillary-strengthening, and photoprotective properties. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its extraordinary anthocyanin antioxidant density.

Valine is an essential branched-chain amino acid used in skincare as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent, contributing to the skin's natural moisturizing factor (NMF) and moisture retention. It is biocompatible, non-irritating, and well-tolerated across all skin types.

Vanilla isolates are standardized extracts or isolated active compounds from vanilla (Vanilla planifolia) pods providing vanillin and polyphenol antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging properties and a characteristic aromatic quality. They are generally well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations; individuals with fragrance sensitivity should be aware that vanillin can act as a contact sensitizer in some formulations.

Vanilla planifolia fruit extract is vanilla bean extract providing vanillin, polyphenols, and ferulic acid with antioxidant and skin-conditioning properties alongside a characteristic vanilla fragrance note. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations; individuals with fragrance sensitivity should note the aromatic vanillin content.

Vegetable oil is a non-specific label term indicating the formula contains one or more plant-derived triglyceride oils — the exact oil(s) are not identified. Skin compatibility, comedogenic potential, and emollient properties depend entirely on which specific oil(s) are used; this lack of specificity limits ingredient transparency.

Vigna radiata (mung bean) seed extract provides polyphenol antioxidants, isoflavones, and skin-conditioning compounds with free-radical-scavenging and brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its antioxidant content.

Vinyl dimethicone is a reactive vinyl-terminated dimethicone used as a crosslinking building block in silicone elastomer systems and as a film-former in cosmetics, contributing to smooth, flexible silicone films with long-wear properties. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Vinyl dimethicone/methicone silsesquioxane crosspolymer is a hybrid crosslinked silicone combining a flexible dimethicone backbone with a rigid methicone silsesquioxane resin, used as a soft-focus texture agent in primers and foundations for a blurring, skin-smoothing effect. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Vinylidene chloride copolymer is a synthetic vinyl-based film-former used in cosmetics to provide durable, water-resistant coatings in color cosmetics. It is considered well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations, functioning as a barrier film-former rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Vitamin C is a generic listing for L-ascorbic acid or one of its stabilized derivatives (sodium ascorbyl phosphate, ascorbyl glucoside, ascorbyl tetraisopalmitate, etc.) — the exact active form may vary by formula. In any form, vitamin C provides antioxidant protection, collagen-stimulating activity, and brightening; the potency, irritation profile, and stability depend significantly on which specific derivative is used.

The trailing parenthesis is a data artifact from ingredient label parsing; this is Vitis vinifera fruit cell extract — grape plant cell extract providing resveratrol, anthocyanins, and proanthocyanidin antioxidants with potent free-radical-scavenging and anti-aging properties. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its high-quality plant-cell-derived antioxidant content.

Vitis vinifera (grape) fruit extract provides resveratrol, anthocyanins, and polyphenol antioxidants with potent free-radical-scavenging, anti-inflammatory, and UV-protective properties. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its exceptional resveratrol and anthocyanin antioxidant content.

Vitis vinifera (grapeseed) seed oil is a light, non-greasy plant oil rich in linoleic acid (~70%) and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) with emollient, antioxidant, and barrier-supporting properties. It is well-tolerated and non-comedogenic, particularly suited to oily, acne-prone, and mixed skin types; its high polyunsaturated fatty acid content makes it prone to oxidation without antioxidant protection.

Vitreoscilla ferment is a ferment derived from Vitreoscilla bacteria uniquely adapted to low-oxygen environments, providing a bacterial hemoglobin-like protein that enhances skin cell oxygen utilization efficiency in the stratum corneum. It is well-tolerated and used in formulas targeting fatigued or oxygen-deprived skin for its oxygenating and barrier-supporting ferment properties.

VP/hexadecene copolymer is a copolymer of vinylpyrrolidone (VP) and hexadecene (a C16 alpha-olefin) used as a film-former and rheology modifier in cosmetics, providing a flexible, adherent film that improves formula texture and wear. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Water — listed as 'Water' rather than the INCI standard 'Aqua' — is the primary solvent in water-based skincare formulas, serving as the vehicle in which all water-soluble ingredients are dissolved or suspended. It is, by definition, inert and universally well-tolerated.

White tea is the common name for minimally processed young buds and leaves of Camellia sinensis, providing catechins (EGCG), theaflavins, and polyphenol antioxidants with exceptional free-radical-scavenging and photoprotective properties — often in higher concentrations than green tea due to minimal processing. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its extraordinary polyphenol antioxidant density.

White willow bark extract is the common name for Salix alba bark extract, providing salicin, polyphenol antioxidants, and tannins with anti-inflammatory, astringent, and mild exfoliant properties from its natural salicylate content. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations; individuals with aspirin or salicylate sensitivity should approach with caution.

Xanthan gum is a microbially produced polysaccharide (from Xanthomonas campestris fermentation) widely used as a natural thickener and stabilizer in water-based skincare formulas, providing smooth, elegant gel textures with excellent shear-thinning rheology. It is considered well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and biodegradable — a preferred natural thickener in clean-formula skincare.

Xylitol is a sugar alcohol derived from birch or corn, used in skincare as a mild humectant that draws moisture to the skin surface and as a mild antimicrobial agent that disrupts biofilm formation. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in moisturizing and conditioning formulas for its gentle, biocompatible humectant properties.

Xylitylglucoside is a mild glucoside humectant derived from xylitol and glucose, used in skincare for its water-binding and skin-conditioning properties alongside mild surfactant character that contributes to formula cleanability. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Yeast extract (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) provides amino acids, B vitamins, beta-glucans, and nucleic acid-derived compounds with skin-conditioning, brightening, and barrier-supporting properties. It is well-tolerated and used in nourishing and antioxidant formulas for its bioavailable nutrient content.

Yeast ferment extract is the extract from fermented Saccharomyces cerevisiae yeast providing concentrated bioactive metabolites, B vitamins, amino acids, and polysaccharides with skin-brightening, conditioning, and barrier-supporting properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and ferment-forward formulas for its concentrated bioactive fermentation content.

Yellow 5 (FD&C Yellow No. 5, Tartrazine) is a synthetic azo dye used as a colorant in cosmetics providing bright yellow pigmentation. It is approved for cosmetic use; individuals with tartrazine sensitivity — which can co-occur with aspirin sensitivity or atopy — may experience contact reactions and should approach formulas containing this dye with caution.

This entry combines a product shade name ('Yellow 6 Fleur:') with an ingredient listing; diisostearyl malate is a polyfunctional ester emollient used in lip products and color cosmetics for its non-sticky, cushioning skin feel and excellent pigment dispersion properties. Diisostearyl malate is well-tolerated and non-irritating.
